name: @chbrain/khai-engine-hope
description: khai engine content, the hope domain: hope as position, the persona's resting orientation toward what the future holds -- across the two axes of expected valence and felt agency.
license: SEE LICENSE IN LICENSE and LICENSE-CODE

khai.tagline: Hope as position: the persona's resting orientation toward what the future holds -- the slant brought to every horizon before an outcome arrives -- across the two axes of expected valence and felt agency.

card.wire: Attaches at Position. The anchor (position_hope.md) declares hope as a resting orientation toward the future -- a baseline on two axes, the expected future good or bad and the persona's hand on it felt or absent -- and the system-wide law makes every persona carry it beneath every plan.
card.issue: Four ready expressions a persona may hold, the four quadrants of valence by agency: hopeful (good expected, high agency -- will plus route), optimistic (good expected, low agency -- it will work out), dread (bad expected, low agency -- braced and unable to steer), and defiant (bad expected, high agency -- grit against the odds). Each is a full position file the build composes onto the anchor, so the persona gets an orientation that colours every horizon, not a label. More expressions slot in as sibling files without moving the anchor.
card.require: Two altitudes must hold. One, the law: declared once in the project's Instructions Knowledge chapter ('personas carry a hope'), so the orientation holds system-wide and no persona meets a future on neutral ground. Two, the link: each persona carries its hope under Projection. A persona may also hold a different, even contradicting, orientation under Shadow; the gap between the hope shown and the hope masked is the character, not a contradiction to reject.
card.enforce: Structure is checked; coherence is judged. The kit verifies the floor: the law is declared, and each persona carries a hope under Projection. Whether a Projection hope and a Shadow hope cohere into a true persona is semantic, so it is taught and reviewed, never failed by the suite. The engine ships its own structural tests; the reading is guided, not gated.
card.setup: Two steps. Declare the law in the Instructions Knowledge chapter, linking the anchor. Then give each persona its hope under Projection: one expression, which carries the anchor upward. Where a persona masks a different orientation, put it under Shadow. Read it in how the persona leans toward the future; the room reads it beneath the plans, never narrates it.
